name: First message contains both a job request AND name signal — quote wins, ask is skipped
description: User opens with 'Quote a fence for the Patels — $350' on a brand-new account. The ask short-circuit checks looksLikeJobRequest() before firing; the message has a quote signal ($) so we skip the ask entirely and route to the LLM. The user's name stays unset for now — Bossie can re-ask on a future conversation when there's no in-flight request.

persona.name: Impatient contractor
persona.role: Knows what they want; doesn't want a chat
persona.context: Just wants to draft a quote in two seconds.

steps.action: Types 'Quote a fence repair for the Patels — $350' and hits Enter
steps.expect: looksLikeJobRequest=true (dollar sign + 5+ words). Assistant produces a create_quote action_card directly. NO 'Hey 👋 quick one' ask appears.
Quote locks; email queued. Onboarding ask still hasn't fired.
On this fresh thread, Bossie now fires the onboarding ask (history.length === 1, user.name still null)

evaluationCriteria.check: No interruption modal between the user's request and the action card
Power user is rewarded — they don't get blocked on a name prompt for a clear job request
looksLikeJobRequest catches the $ and word-count >= 5 cases; trade vocabulary (fence, deck, roof, etc.) also bails out
No interrupting focus jump — first action card lands as expected
